data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of 59ef's premise (the dual-run surface is real, not fictional): all four enrichment methods 59ef's harness contracts resolve on the real AcliClient with the exact referenced signatures — search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) (acli.py:370-375), get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289-293), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py:189). get_comment_map returns the RAW nested Jira `comment` field {"comments":[...], "total":N} (docstring acli_graph.py:303-308) — the exact bug-0ee6 seam — so contracting the fake's return shape against the real client's is grounded. Existing production-path fakes already use these signatures (tests/unit/.../mutate/test_comment_enrichment.py:196, test_link_sync.py:163), confirming the surface is real and in use. No sibling moves/renames these methods.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef adds tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_acliclient_contract.py and only READS acli_graph.py; it CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient (_fakes.py) and the real AcliClient. fe57 BUILDS the fake, 59ef VERIFIES it — clean producer/verifier split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes 59ef's real-vs-fake shape contract. No representation mismatch on the inbound enrichment surface (the outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter on 2359/33d0 is outbound-internal and untouched here).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake for the hermetic leg). Fixture capture (→fe57) and snapshot round-trip (→8937) are correctly scoped OUT; the harness stands up without 8937's schema or 822a's probe. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct, complementary surfaces.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only ordering constraint (fake before its verifier) is captured by depends_on fe57. 59ef is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean"); AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on any consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four contracted methods exist on the real client (verified above); the referenced fake is fe57's artifact. 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) is a G3 COVERAGE matter — and per the plan's verbatim AC it is explicitly owned by story B (8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
